title: Governmet to spend bn on quantum computing
why:
- The government announced £2bn investment in quantum computing research over the next four years.
- Companies will be encouraged to build prototype quantum computers, aiming for commercial versions within the next decade.
- Targeted applications include financial services, quantum navigation, energy, and pharmaceuticals.
- The initiative aims to create more than 100,000 jobs within two decades and potentially deliver £212bn in economic impact.
- A new programme, ProQure: Scaling UK Quantum Computing, will invite companies to propose and develop state-of-the-art quantum prototypes for evaluation and scale.
- Recent technical advancements include the delivery of a 100-qubit quantum computer at the National Quantum Computing Centre and the launch of the IonQ Quantum Innovation Centre with a 256-qubit system.
